idea: Replace vision as the primary world-model observation with contact-microphone audio and proprioception, and test whether "audible dynamics" suffice for latent-dynamics prediction in contact-rich manipulation.
reason: Audio world models (2512.08405) and world-model modality ablations (2606.13877) jointly cover most of the methodology, capping novelty; contact-sound fidelity in data/simulation remains uncertain, but the specific framing around phase-dependent visual redundancy is unoccupied, and the falsification experiment is feasible on 1×H100.

idea: Define actions in tactile space: the policy predicts desired contact-state changes (normal force/slip/contact location), and a low-level tactile servo closes the loop, decoupling the action representation from embodiment.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): The headline "contact/force-space action interface, low-level servo, and cross-embodiment transfer" is directly occupied by 2606.15516 and FeelTheForce (2506.01944); the remaining increment only specifies the tactile-state vocabulary and cannot establish a clear-accept-level distinction within the lifecycle.

idea: Treat a VLA's open-loop action chunk as speculative execution, using a jointly predicted force/tactile envelope as a "physical assertion" that triggers abort, rollback, and replanning (pipeline flush) when measurements cross its bounds.
reason: The "chunk as speculative execution with assertion and abort" framework is occupied by SV-VLA (2604.02965) and 2603.19418, capping novelty; the minimal falsification experiment also compares only a weak fixed-chunk baseline and omits strong RDP/SV-VLA-line baselines (F3). The specific combination of force assertions with kHz low-level validation was not found, and the experiment is feasible and falsifiable.

idea: Use audio tracks from internet human videos to label "contact moments" automatically, providing structured pseudo-supervision for manipulation pretraining and injecting contact structure into video representations.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): RepLAI (2209.13583) fully implements the headline mechanism, "audio track as a free contact-moment labeler for video-representation pretraining," on the same data sources (Ego4D/EPIC); the remaining increment merely changes the downstream task to manipulation BC, transferring a known mechanism to a new domain.

idea: Quantify, phase by phase, how much information vision/force/proprioception each provide about the optimal action; formalize a "modality switch point" and test whether vision is nearly redundant during contact.
reason: The reported prior-work search omits the adjacent human perceptual-motor-control/cue integration literature, making it weak (MAJOR). Single-modality probe predictability≠conditional mutual information, and modality correlation confounds the switch-point definition (MAJOR). No direct robotics precedent was found for the per-timestep quantification framework, and the probe experiment is feasible and falsifiable within hours.

idea: Detect precursors to failure from geometric statistics of the π0.5 flow-matching denoising trajectory, enabling safe early stopping before action execution.
reason: Failure detection from intrinsic generative signals is crowded; Sentinel (2410.04640) sampling dispersion and denoising geometry may measure the same phenomenon, capping novelty. The falsification experiment directly tests the increment against final-action statistics, has a sound design, and is feasible in several days on 1×H100.

idea: Apply psychometric item response theory to embodied benchmarks: estimate each task's discrimination and difficulty, remove uninformative tasks, and construct a short adaptive evaluation.
reason: The mechanism (difficulty-and-discrimination estimation with adaptive-subset rank reproduction) is fully developed by tinyBenchmarks/ATLAS/2603.23749 in the LLM and agent domains, while 2606.04233 explicitly identifies embodiment as future work, leaving only domain transfer (MAJOR). With 2PL IRT and only ~10 policies as ability points, discrimination parameters may be unidentifiable, and comparisons against random subsets may be drowned by noise (MAJOR).

idea: Quantify the "noise floor" of VLA evaluation success rates by decomposing test-retest variance on the same checkpoint, then measure how many reported performance gaps fall below evaluation noise.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): The headline finding that most reported gaps lack statistical significance was already quantified by benchmark in 2606.04233 (the prior-work review rated the overlap as high), so the nearest work directly dominates the idea. The remaining increment (test-retest variance decomposition and MDE lookup) has too low a ceiling for a standalone paper.

idea: Define a training-free "transferability score" for cross-embodiment robot data that predicts which heterogeneous data actually benefit a target embodiment without training a large model.
reason: Validation uses only n=6-8 datasets; Spearman≥0.7 has little statistical power at that sample size, while a multi-component score has enough freedom to overfit 8 points (MAJOR). Using simulated Franka as the target and real-robot data as sources confounds measured gains with both the sim gap and embodiment transfer, limiting transfer back to the real target (MAJOR). Although overlap is low, shallow reads and the workshop boundary keep novelty capped.

idea: Replace "predict accurately" with "judge correctly" as the world-model training objective: use policy-ranking fidelity as an explicit loss to create a world model specialized as an offline evaluator.
reason: The ranking loss requires ground-truth environment returns from many policies as training labels, which are precisely the cost offline evaluation seeks to avoid; label acquisition is unspecified (MAJOR). The 10-20 checkpoints from different stages of the same training run are highly correlated, so ranking fidelity may not generalize to unseen policy families and the evaluator may overfit (MAJOR). WM-as-evaluator and VAML also constrain the space; the increment is only the loss form, with motivation already published in 2606.15032.

idea: Inject random asynchronous delays into each modality during training and provide the delay as a conditioning token, teaching the VLA to "know how stale its sensors are" for latency robustness and degraded-hardware operation.
reason: The delay-simulation and conditioning scaffold is occupied by TT-RTC (2605.08168) and 2512.05964; on a standard VLA testbed (only RGB and proprioception), per-modality asynchrony nearly collapses to the existing global-delay setting (MAJOR). Quasi-static simulated manipulation may be insensitive to 100-300 ms stale observations; if the baseline does not degrade, the signal floor is too low and the falsification experiment may be vacuous (MAJOR).

idea: Repair the phase-wise modality-information framework by replacing single-modality probes with interventional masking and conditional-mutual-information estimation, producing an identifiable "modality switch point."
reason: On expert trajectories, masked-probe CMI measures imitation information for "expert actions under the expert-state distribution," not the claimed "information about optimal actions"; the estimand and conclusion are misaligned (MAJOR). The phenomenon itself (a shift toward force during contact) was observed qualitatively in 2509.23468; the identifiable formalization is a real contribution, but its ceiling is borderline, the radar has no 8+ dimension, and it does not meet the SA threshold.

idea: Embed a several-hundred-Hz proprioception/force "reflex pathway" beneath a slow visual VLA backbone, continuously correcting actions during chunk execution; train the reflex layer jointly with the backbone instead of relying on a handcrafted impedance fallback.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): The headline "slow VLA backbone with high-frequency force-driven small-network within-chunk residual correction" architecture is covered by FAVLA (2602.23648)/PhaForce (2603.08342)/RDP (2503.02881)/MRC (2603.15152) (the prior-work review rated the overlap as high). The residual differences—proprioception-only input, a 100-500 Hz update range, and joint training—are configuration-level and do not overcome the prior-work dominance.

idea: Use only the proprioception/torque streams already recorded in large teleoperation datasets to mine collision, slip, and drop events offline, producing failure labels for existing corpora at scale and at no new collection cost.
reason: The open combination (pure-proprioception offline retrospective failure mining for DROID/OXE-scale corpora) is valid and the falsification experiment is feasible, but both halves of the mechanism are occupied (2509.26308 establishes force/proprioception anomaly detection; SCIZOR/FIDeL cover automatic labeling of large offline corpora). This scales a known mechanism into a new domain, and torque/current availability across OXE subsets is unverified by the reported search; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Add a sparse force/torque decoding auxiliary loss to a video world model that is active "only at contact-event moments," and test whether <1% additional supervision can disproportionately repair contact-dynamics errors in prediction.
reason: The headline direction, "force/contact supervision in video world models," is occupied by the dense variants FAWAM/ChronoDreamer/VTAM, capping novelty. The residual "sparse event triggering with <1% supervision leverage" hypothesis is unvalidated and can be killed by the falsification experiment, but the leverage claim requires a dense-control arm and has the ceiling of a supervision-budget ablation.

idea: Formalize action-chunk length as a trade-off between "replanning-latency benefit vs open-loop drift cost under environmental stochasticity," derive how the optimum scales with measurable environment statistics, and test it with probe experiments.
reason: Robot learning lacks a quantitative theory of optimal chunk length, but intermittent-control theory in human motor control (Gawthrop 2011) quantifies the same trade-off. The formalization is a cross-domain transfer, capping novelty; the falsification experiment is feasible and the signal is falsifiable, with a ceiling around a solid poster rather than an oral.

idea: Build a retrieval-augmented world model that queries similar transitions from the deployment environment's recent interaction buffer before prediction, using them as context for gradient-free adaptation to new object dynamics.
reason: Dynamics-as-Prompts (2410.20357) already provides "interaction context for gradient-free dynamics adaptation" with real-robot validation; retrieval and the adaptation target are engineering-level variants (MAJOR). The minimal falsification experiment compares only against an ordinary world model and omits a recent-history-context baseline, so any gain cannot be attributed to the novel retrieval component (MAJOR); 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Refine cross-embodiment data transferability scores from dataset level to episode level, then test whether they predict data utility at thousand-sample scale with leave-one-embodiment-out validation on real corpora.
reason: Episode-level data valuation is crowded (DemInf/CUPID), and Re-Mix already covers OXE utility weighting. The remaining combination of a "cross-embodiment transferability target with a leave-one-embodiment-out protocol" is novel only at the combination level (MAJOR). Within real corpora, "target-embodiment performance" can only use an offline proxy weakly correlated with actual performance, so selection effects may be lost in noise (MAJOR); 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Use time-reversal symmetry as free physical supervision for manipulation data: reverse quasi-static segments that remain physically feasible, train the VLA with reversal augmentation, and use reversal-consistency residuals to label irreversible events automatically.
reason: The core mechanism is occupied in parts—PvP produces "reverse-task demonstrations with pick↔place swapping" (BC/real robot), while TR-DRL provides "reversal augmentation with dynamics-consistency reversibility filtering." The residual combination, "transfer to the VLA domain with an AUROC output form," collides at F1 and is CRITICAL. TSDA also shows that reversal augmentation degrades substantially in realistic settings that violate reversibility, adding 1 MAJOR issue.

idea: Attach a self-supervised geometric-consistency auxiliary head (cross-view relative pose/temporal reprojection) to a frozen VLA visual encoder, using deployment-time spikes in auxiliary error to monitor perception health and stop early.
reason: The mechanism precedent (SSL loss as an OOD score, NeurIPS'19) and the observation-side FIPER baseline each undermine the motivation that existing detectors are mostly action-side (MAJOR). A geometric-consistency auxiliary head dedicated to perceptual fidelity had zero hits across the VLA search surface (14 papers read); the minimal falsification experiment is feasible and falsifiable, with 2 MAJOR issues capping the result.

idea: Transfer "video coding for machines" to robot corpora: allocate lossy-compression bitrate by policy relevance and test whether fleet-scale storage can fall by an order of magnitude without reducing policy performance.
reason: The VCM×policy training combination has zero arXiv hits, but LeRobot reports that "standard encoding compresses to ~14% without reducing policy performance," leaving uncertain headroom for policy-aware bitrate allocation (MAJOR). Only 3 papers were read, and the industrial fleet search is weak (MAJOR). The falsification experiment is cheap and genuinely falsifiable; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Refine retrieval world models around cross-episode lifelong memory, and compare directly against a strong recent-history-context baseline so gains can be attributed to retrieval itself.
reason: EMWM (NeurIPS'22 wksp) occupies the core mechanism of "cross-episode nonparametric transition memory assisting world-model prediction"; the difference is motivation and setting rather than mechanism (MAJOR). The interleaved task stream is tailored to retrieval without evidence from real deployment streams, suggesting F9 (MAJOR). The attribution design and recent-history-context baseline are sound; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Probe whether Euclidean flow-matching action heads fail in rotation-heavy manipulation because they ignore SO(3) manifold structure, using a geodesic action head as the controlled comparison.
reason: LDA (2606.01847) already occupies the headline as a method paper: it names the VLA action head's "Euclidean Fallacy" and shows gains from a manifold-correct version on CALVIN and real robots. The probe's bottleneck question has therefore been answered; the residual off-manifold quantification and failure-correlation attribution cannot sustain a paper (F1 CRITICAL), while an A-tier probe would require a surprising finding (1 MAJOR)

idea: Test whether compression (quantization/pruning) disproportionately erases a VLA's long-tail recovery and safety behavior, causing perturbed recovery to collapse before nominal success rates move.
reason: The mechanism is isomorphic to 1911.05248 (compression harms the long tail), making the headline a transfer of a known phenomenon into a closed-loop domain. A simulation-side perturbation-recovery protocol has no established precedent and must be created, leaving its validity open to challenge; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Test whether the optimal policy model shrinks under domain shift: smaller models may become more robust as shift grows, yielding a "shift-size" selection law for edge deployment.
reason: The premise has direct counterevidence in the nearest domain (2112.04094 finds larger models better on worst-group performance). A reversal trained from scratch across a 1M-100M family would be attributed to textbook bias-variance rather than a new finding, and the experiment cannot support the claimed deployment-level "shift-size selection law" (F6). Adding a pretrained VLA-scale model family exceeds a defensible single-researcher 1×H100 budget; >2 directional defects remain.

idea: Move capacity from parameters into state: use tiny weights and a large writable recurrent state so a VLA improves through accumulated state without gradient updates during deployment, targeting edge-memory budgets.
reason: Memory-VLA work is dense, but only 3 papers were read and several named neighbors (MAP-VLA/EchoVLA/ReMem-VLA) were not, leaving novelty unverified. The minimal falsification experiment also lacks KV-cache/long-context ICL baselines needed to distinguish the core claim from existing in-context adaptation; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Measure the "action information rate" (bits/s) a task truly requires, testing whether action decoding can be nearly parameter-free and a tiny VLA should allocate almost all capacity to perception.
reason: The conditional entropy rate of demonstrations upper-bounds behavioral diversity rather than the "task-required information rate," so the headline and measurement are misaligned. The reported search also omits the direct Fitts' law/human-motor-control information-rate lineage, leaving novelty unverified; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Train a world model on paired rollouts coupled with common random numbers to predict "the outcome difference between two actions" directly, making action evaluation cheaper and more accurate than absolute-future prediction.
reason: A trainable Δ-model applies where simulation seeds are controllable, precisely where direct CRN evaluation makes it least necessary; approximate coupling in the real world is only narrative. The search read only 3 papers and did not cover the OR literature where CRN originated, so it falls below SA-grade prior-work standards; 2 MAJOR issues cap the result.

idea: Time-share one VLA server across multiple robots, using remaining action-chunk duration as scheduling slack to propose and test a new "fleet-brain scheduling" problem.
reason: CRITICAL F1: Kairos (2605.11381) already covers "multiple robots sharing an inference server with execution-aware scheduling," while ROSA (2607.01088) covers fleet-level metrics. The headline claim of a new problem is false, and the remaining gap is only one scheduling signal whose prior use remains unverified.

idea: Replace binary success rate as the embodied-evaluation estimand with a censored time-to-completion survival function, gaining severalfold statistical power under the same rollout budget.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29710.

idea: Hold the training recipe and evaluation fixed, make the data-collection pipeline itself the evaluated object, and establish a DataComp-style data benchmark protocol for robot manipulation.
reason: The empirical core is split between MimicLabs and 2503.07017, and prior-work review records the remaining protocol-level difference as "insufficient," capping novelty. The falsification signal requires a second RoboCasa grid, while the budget lists only 12 LIBERO runs, so the stated experiment cannot falsify itself.

idea: Systematically measure train-evaluation contamination in VLA evaluation: quantify overlap between pretraining corpora and benchmark tasks, then measure how much overlap inflates leaderboard scores.
reason: LIBERO-PRO already occupies the qualitative headline, reducing surprise and capping novelty. Overlap strata are confounded by task difficulty/domain, and SimplerEnv lacks ≥10 tasks per stratum, making the proposed stratified arm infeasible.

idea: Create a known-order policy ladder through calibrated degradation, unit-test the validity of embodied-benchmark metrics and tasks, and produce an "evaluation validity certificate."
reason: Mechanism precedents exist across gridworld, NLG, and OPE, while nontrivial closed-loop adaptation is unproven, capping novelty. The "known-order" foundation is unreliable: small noise may be harmless, low τ cannot distinguish a task defect from a ladder defect, and the full rollout matrix underestimates compute by severalfold.

idea: Turn "blind before sighted" into a VLA curriculum: mask vision early and learn contact structure from proprioception and force, then anneal visual dropout probability from 1 to 0; test whether modality introduction order suppresses visual shortcuts and improves robustness under distribution shift.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): The headline mechanism, "weaken vision early and anneal its intensity to zero to suppress visual shortcuts," is covered by FACTR (2502.17432) and independently confirmed by CRAFT (2602.12532). The prior-work review judges high overlap, leaving only systematic ablation and measurement, insufficient for a clear-accept-level difference; there is also 1 MAJOR (F3: the reported strongest baseline is constant modality dropout, omitting the true nearest neighbor FACTR).

idea: Measure the "substitution elasticity" between visual-only and tactile demonstrations: fit iso-performance curves over a modality-mixture grid, determine how many visual-only demonstrations equal one force/tactile demonstration, and provide a quantitative basis for fleet sensor budgets.
reason: The formulation (iso-performance curves over modality mixtures and substitution elasticity) is unoccupied under independent prior-work review, but exchange-rate/mixture scans are mature across sim-real, human-video/teleoperation, and LLM-data-mixture axes. Near transfer across axes caps novelty (MAJOR), while the "fleet sensor budget" motivation relies on sim-to-real extrapolation that cannot be validated in phase-1 (F6, MAJOR). The minimal falsification experiment is executable, has a clear and conservative kill signal, and therefore remains borderline.

idea: Factor the visual-inertial-odometry pattern "fast-path integration with slow-path loop-closure correction" into a manipulation world model: proprioception/force dead-reckons latent state at millisecond frequency and exposes drift uncertainty, while vision provides low-frequency corrections on demand, reducing visual frame rate by an order of magnitude.
reason: OmniVTA (2603.19201) occupies the main structure and efficiency claim, "high-frequency proprioception/touch with low-frequency vision and rollout that avoids visual-frame generation," capping novelty (MAJOR). The reported strongest baselines (single-rate WM, dropout, Clockwork-VAE, and KVAE) omit an OmniVTA-style fixed-schedule slow-fast WM (F3, MAJOR). The residual "drift uncertainty triggers on-demand visual correction" has zero search hits and an executable falsification experiment, yielding a borderline narrowed headline.

idea: Use a path-speed-decoupled action representation: BC learns geometric paths from canonical-speed demonstrations reparameterized by arc length, while an independent speed controller sets speed online at deployment, making execution speed an in-distribution control.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00110.

idea: Propose a closed-loop "latency-capacity" scaling law: on fixed edge hardware, larger models consume staler observations; predict and measure an optimal model-size crossover in dynamic manipulation whose position moves predictably with environment speed.
reason: Novelty is capped. Reference: 2412.14355 identifies the chain from model size through latency to stochastic-environment cost in games, and that paper plus RTC's asynchronous-execution result directly violate the idea's survival condition. The residual quantitative crossover in manipulation is insufficient for clear accept, and 2 MAJOR findings cap the result.

idea: Use manipulation-phase-driven expert paging for MoE-VLA: exploit temporal locality in expert activation induced by episode phases, prefetch experts with a proprioceptive phase predictor, and keep only 1/4 of experts resident in edge memory with almost no added latency.
reason: Both sides of the mechanism are heavily occupied (PAMAE supplies a VLA phase-prediction head, while EdgeMoE/MoE-Infinity/MoE-Beyond cover learned prefetch paging). The residual is only source and domain substitution, and LAR-MoE qualitatively covers the core assumption, triggering the stated kill condition. At 100-200M scale, edge-memory pressure is also absent because a few hundred MB already fit; even a fully realized signal has a ceiling below borderline, leaving novelty doubtful.

idea: Measure how failure predictability scales with policy size, testing whether "smaller-model failures are more predictable" and whether a "tiny policy with a failure monitor and retry" matches a large standalone model's system reliability at 1/10 of the compute.
reason: Nature 2024 (larger models are harder to warn about) and 2408.03314 (small model with test-time compute matches a large model) split the conceptual headline in the LLM domain. Robot-domain AUROC-size scaling under matched FLOPs is substantive, but the prior-work review finds it insufficient for clear accept, capping novelty below SA.

idea: Success tolerances in contact tasks differ by orders of magnitude along constraint-normal and tangential directions, while flow-matching action heads allocate denoising precision uniformly with isotropic noise; formalize this precision mismatch and test contact-geometry-shaped noise metrics.
reason: The problem formulation is already occupied by 2602.06339 (the isotropic precision barrier), while 2606.18186 occupies noise-covariance shaping. The residual "contact geometry as the noise-metric source with normal-direction failure evidence" is unoccupied and has a single-GPU executable falsification experiment, but its ceiling is borderline and novelty remains capped below SA.

idea: Actions inside an action chunk are open loop to instantaneous state, cutting the confounding path from demonstrator latent intent; treat chunked robot data as a quasi-experiment to quantify and reduce policy-confounding bias learned by world models.
reason: Open-loop chunks provide quasi-experimental identification and measured manipulation-domain confounding are unoccupied (low overlap), with an executable experiment and clear kill signal. The theoretical backbone is occupied by 2002.02836/2110.10819, and all phase-1 evidence uses scripted synthetic confounding, compressing the increment to the intersection and leaving a borderline ceiling.

idea: Distill the free post-hoc open-loop/closed-loop divergence curve in training data into a per-state "commitment horizon" head, allowing a deployed VLA to choose chunk length and replanning time adaptively by state.
reason: The prior-work review finds high overlap: DEHP (2606.11408) covers the headline deliverable (per-state execution-horizon head, frozen base, zero runtime overhead), while AutoHorizon/PACE/A3/DVAC fill the test-time line. With 8 papers occupying the space within six months, the "new problem" framing fails; the remaining supervision-source difference reads as an offline-supervision variant of DEHP and does not reach borderline.

idea: Treat bimanual manipulation as out-of-order execution: objects are registers, manipulation primitives are instructions, and a scoreboard detects read/write conflicts online; each arm runs chunks asynchronously and pauses only on conflict, replacing lockstep synchronization in bimanual VLAs.
reason: The two headline pillars are separately occupied (asynchronous execution in RTC/PAINT/ScheduleStream and online conflict detection with pausing in CoFreeVLA). The increment narrows to their combination and does not reach a clear-accept-level difference, capping novelty; the falsification experiment remains executable, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

idea: Teleoperation demonstrations contain several hundred milliseconds of human reaction latency, but BC treats (o_t,a_t) as aligned and learns a policy convolved with a latency kernel; formulate deconvolution and jointly estimate the latency distribution with a debiased policy objective.
reason: The problem "human reaction latency contaminates BC labels and requires temporal debiasing" is occupied by 1912.02241/2004.00981/2312.06670; the remaining increment is only distributional formalization with no empirical-value evidence (MAJOR). Uncertain FABG numbering and unread sensorimotor precursors make the prior-work review weak, adding a second MAJOR and capping the idea.

idea: Measure policy-performance decay along continuous interpolation paths through a parameterized embodiment space, test for non-smooth cross-embodiment "transfer cliffs," and determine whether measurable geometry such as workspace overlap predicts cliff locations.
reason: The headline (continuous-morphology cliffs with geometric predictors) is unoccupied among fully read neighbors and has the highest ceiling, but only 5 papers were read, 2603.00182 was not verified, and coverage was self-rated shallow. The strict rule caps unverified novelty pending stronger prior-work review.

idea: Apply Buckingham's π theorem to manipulation policies: organize states and actions into dimensionless π groups, learn the policy in π space, and transfer zero-shot across object scale, mass, and gravity under physical similarity laws.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.08768.

idea: The standard account of long-horizon world-model rollout drift is compounded single-step error variance; an alternative is directional systematic bias induced by the training objective. Use cross-seed bias-variance decomposition to identify the actual error term.
reason: 2504.01766 formalizes which error term multi-step objectives affect, with a theoretical result (multi-step training under misspecification reduces bias) opposite the expected signal; long-horizon systematic-bias correction is also routine in weather, capping novelty. The "training-objective-induced" attribution is confounded with mean collapse under stochasticity, and the minimal experiment has no separating control; these 2 MAJOR findings cap the idea, although the experiment itself is executable and falsifiable, leaving it near borderline.

idea: The standard explanation for flat gains from larger VLA backbones is a data bottleneck; an alternative is that the number of interface tokens between backbone and action head is the information bottleneck. Under matched compute, widening the interface should improve more than enlarging the backbone.
reason: The qualitative premise "the interface is an information bottleneck" is occupied in-domain by PVI (2603.12772), while MM1 demonstrates the same conclusion in an adjacent domain, leaving only a quantitative two-dimensional scan and capping novelty. Matching FLOPs across heterogeneous axes is difficult, and with 3 seeds the expected interaction may be indistinguishable from evaluation noise; these 2 MAJOR findings cap the idea.

idea: Long-horizon task failures concentrate at subtask boundaries; rather than OOD inputs or compounded error, chunk sampling and padding in the training pipeline may systematically undertrain boundary states, making failure maps predictable from supervision-coverage maps.
reason: The same boundary artifact already has causal-intervention evidence under an inference-noise attribution (2603.11642), while the minimal experiment lacks a mechanism-separation arm and may have little remaining effect. The coverage mechanism also requires subtask boundaries to align with segment ends, which is unlikely for continuous LIBERO-Long demonstrations, halving the evidence base; these 2 MAJOR findings cap it, though a static audit can falsify it cheaply.

idea: Quantify a systematic "post-failure dynamics" blind spot in video world models: success-biased corpora prevent models from imagining the world after drops, topples, or collisions; test whether a small amount of internet failure video repairs the blind spot.
reason: MiraBench systematically evaluates the headline phenomenon that success-biased WMs cannot represent failures, while Scalable-Policy-Eval bounds the failure-data repair direction, yielding 2 MAJOR findings. A shared-prefix paired quantification protocol with equal-token attribution for internet failure video was not found, and the minimal falsification experiment is executable and falsifiable.

idea: Diagnose what "latent actions" learned from internet video encode: test whether camera ego-motion consumes most capacity rather than hand-object interaction, then repair it through hand-centered stabilization and re-encoding.
reason: The target paper LAPA acknowledges the headline (camera motion is encoded by latent actions), UniVLA completes diagnosis, repair, and downstream validation in the same framework, and LAOM/LAWM-wild/LA-Pose/VLA-JEPA cover it from four more directions. The remaining quantitative-share protocol cannot support a top-tier paper, leaving F1 indefensible within the lifecycle and therefore CRITICAL.

idea: Decompose a world model into a replaceable "self-motion model" and shared "environment-dynamics core," train them jointly on cross-embodiment data, and adapt to a new embodiment with few samples by replacing only the self module.
reason: The mechanism backbone (self/world decomposition, a shared world core, and replacement of only the self module for a new embodiment) is occupied by Know Thyself, while HMA/Vidar/LAC-WM constrain few-shot new-embodiment capability, yielding 2 MAJOR findings. The combination of a learned self branch, swap consistency, and a leave-one-embodiment random-motion protocol has no single-paper hit, and the falsification experiment is executable.

idea: Treat the success criterion of simulated manipulation benchmarks as the object under test: after release, continue physical settling, apply standard small perturbations, and run penetration/stability rechecks; quantify false-success rates and whether policy rankings reverse under corrected criteria.
reason: The falsification experiment is executable, but SafeVLA-Bench SBU covers the headline that many reported successes are physically unstable. The residual is only settle rechecking and rank reversal, and the prior-work review judges the difference insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty.

idea: Treat embodied reward/VLM-judge metrics as the evaluated object: build a simulated perception-pathology diagnostic set with known truth—interpenetration false completions, occluded true completions, and threshold flicker—and measure each reward signal's false positives, false negatives, and temporal stability rather than policy quality.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap, but the two closest papers (Dark Side 2409.15922/Video-Language Critic 2405.19988) were not opened, and only 4 papers were fully read. Novelty of the core reward FP/FN claim is unverified, so weak prior-work review caps it at MAJOR.

idea: Name and measure "action collapse" in latent-dynamics world models; add a local-data-controllability-weighted action-sensitivity contrastive loss so the WM allocates capacity to controllable dimensions and recovers evaluability/controllability.
reason: The falsification experiment is the most complete, but novelty rests only on "local controllability weighting." Its nearest neighbor, Empowerment Gain (2512.08230, where MI denotes controllability), was not opened, leaving the core component unverified and capping novelty.

idea: Diagnose "replan the whole open-loop chunk whenever a task-irrelevant object moves" as false sharing in the world belief: one monolithic belief lets an unrelated write invalidate everything and cause replanning thrash; object-slot partitioning localizes invalidation, making replanning frequency decline predictably with coupling granularity.
reason: The false-sharing diagnostic headline has low overlap, but mechanism-transfer SA condition 1, zero target-domain hits, is disputed by the localized-replanning neighbor 1911.04577 (the prior-work evidence is explicitly uncertain). Only the Broader score of 8 reaches 8+, failing the two-dimension SA threshold, capping the result at AwR; the falsification experiment is executable.

idea: Replace pixel/latent-state prediction with "affordance-set evolution" as the world-model objective, directly predicting which interactions become feasible or infeasible after each action.
reason: The prior-work review finds high overlap: AGWM (2605.06841) explicitly occupies the headline "affordance-set dynamics as an explicit world-model prediction target." The remaining difference only instantiates discrete-to-continuous domains; the prior-work review judges it insufficient for clear-accept, making the F1 case dominated by prior work and yielding a Reject verdict.

idea: Formalize the overlooked silent VLA failure class of "freezing/idling/no progress," define a task-independent progress-stagnation measure, and build a policy-decoupled detector.
reason: Sentinel (2410.04640) partially occupies "stagnation as an independent failure class," with medium overlap. Three concrete headlines (incidence quantification, proprioception-only input, a time- and task-independent detector, and timeout/entropy baselines) remain uncovered but are Faster-style refinements, with no 8+ dimension, leaving it below SA (F1 MAJOR); the cheapest falsification experiment is executable, so the verdict is AwR.

idea: Pipeline the K-step denoising software in a flow-matching action head: advance one step per tick while staggering multiple future chunks, amortizing K-step latency to near 1 step per tick. The core new problem is silent bias in reactive actions from stale conditional observations between pipeline stages, repaired by per-step conditioning refresh.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): The headline mechanism (pipelined denoising, staggered noise levels, and per-step conditioning refresh) is covered by SDP (2406.04806) alone (high overlap). The residual only analyzes refresh as an independent component of the staleness-throughput frontier, which the prior-work review judges insufficient for clear-accept; the new problem, conditional staleness biasing reactive actions, is also occupied by SDP's stated limitation and A2C2 (2509.23224).

idea: Measure policy-performance decay along continuous interpolation paths through a parameterized embodiment space, test for non-smooth transfer cliffs, and determine whether measurable geometry such as workspace overlap predicts cliff locations.
reason: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: no single paper occupies the full proposition (non-smooth cliffs and prediction of their locations from measurable geometry along continuous morphology changes), but each half is strongly occupied (RichMap 2604.06778 links geometry↔transfer, while humanoid/AnyBody morphology distance↔degradation). The novelty difference is uncertain at clear-accept level; adverse judgment therefore caps it. The minimal falsification experiment (sim parameterized arm family×≥20 points×≥50 rollouts, executable on 1×H100) is valid, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

idea: Use multi-operator teleoperation data to quantify operator-style leakage: policies memorize personal motion style as a task signal, fail systematically when deployment operators differ from dominant operators, and expose how strongly probes decode style internally.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap: no single robotics-BC paper covers "internal probe leakage, leave-one-operator shift, and de-styling," but this transfers a known effect from medical annotation style (2210.17398). Linear probes/adversarial de-styling are established, nontrivial adaptation is uncertain (SA cond2 missing), and the signal ceiling is borderline (cond3 uncertain); DROID/multi-operator ALOHA also creates data risk because labels for ≥6 operators may be unavailable, capping the result at AwR.

idea: Distill dynamics Jacobians analytically provided by differentiable simulation into a learned world model as a first-order consistency loss; test whether matching local dynamics gradients, rather than only next-state prediction, eliminates long-horizon rollout drift in purely predictive world models.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.01178.

idea: Domain randomization holds expert action labels fixed while randomizing dynamics, making the same action suboptimal in randomized instances; formalize DR as action-label covariate-shift noise that grows with randomization strength, then correct it through dynamics-conditioned relabeling.
reason: The headline premise (fixed expert labels become suboptimal under changed dynamics, with dynamics-conditioned correction) is occupied by ADAIL (2008.12647)/2103.05910 (medium overlap). The residual is only formalizing "DR creates strength-modulated label noise" and measuring noise against strength, insufficient for clear-accept; a simulation over 6 levels × 4 seeds (including per-instance MPC relabeling) is executable on 1×H100, so the result is capped at accept-w-rev.

idea: Internet video supplies only observational marginal dynamics, so video-only world models mistake correlation for causation; quantify this spurious-causal bias and test whether a small amount of interactive data with counterfactual null approaches can debias it directionally.
reason: Passive video induces spurious causality and debiasing with interactive/counterfactual data are occupied by WoW (2509.22642)/Causal-Confusion/CAIAC (medium overlap). The residual is only a diagnostic protocol for "paired-probe spurious-causality rate and counterfactual-null approach versus an equal random-interaction control," insufficient for clear-accept. A small WM with 500 pairs in a simulation-probe falsification experiment is executable but engineering-heavy, capping the result at accept-w-rev.

idea: Negative transfer in cross-embodiment co-training may concentrate in contact phases with the largest embodiment-dynamics differences rather than uniformly; decompose negative transfer by manipulation phase and predict its location from phase-level dynamics divergence.
reason: The phase-level negative-transfer decomposition has low overlap, and the prior-work review confirms it is unoccupied (existing ν-gap/2602.18025 work stops at dataset/embodiment/system level), so novelty is not capped. However, this is a pure diagnostic and applies the ν-gap dynamics-divergence predictor at finer granularity; no dimension reaches 8 (Stronger/Broader each 6), while SA requires ≥2 dimensions at 8+; zero-hit confidence is also weakened by SemanticScholar rate limits and narrow recall and unexcluded non-arXiv industry occupation, capping it at AwR (0 MAJOR).

idea: At equal aggregate dynamics error, compare simulators that are systematically biased but self-consistent vs unbiased but high-variance; test whether self-consistent bias transfers better than unbiased noise, challenging the DR claim that more randomization always improves robustness.
reason: The controlled bias-variance decomposition is executable, and no single paper occupies the headline under matched aggregate error. Explicit sim-only scope caps it because treating held-out dynamics as real hardware is only a proxy, while DORAEMON and 2002.11635 separately cover both directional halves, making the result unsurprising and unlikely to reach clear-accept, excluding SA.

idea: Cross-embodiment co-training pads heterogeneous proprioception into a unified schema, causing systematic underuse of proprioception and contact-phase degradation; diagnose this "padding dilution" and repair it with per-embodiment projection alignment.
reason: The minimal falsification experiment is executable, but the repair arm (per-embodiment projection)≡HPT's existing design, capping novelty. The diagnostic premise of systematic proprioception underuse also contradicts the over-reliance finding in 2509.18644, yielding 2 MAJOR findings and capping it at AwR.

idea: In first-person human video, hands are most often self-occluded by objects or leave the frame during contact-dense moments, creating an "action black hole"; quantify the defect and test whether it predicts systematic contact-phase weakness after human-video pretraining.
reason: The phase-specific causal test (black-hole rate vs contact-phase gains, with no relation in noncontact phases) is executable and has no single-paper hit, but the core observation "hand self-occlusion at contact causes fallback to pose priors" is established by 2305.16301/2604.12343; only a modest quantification and attribution increment remains, unlikely to reach clear-accept, excluding SA.

idea: The standard account of poor contact-dynamics prediction in learned world models is missing dense force/tactile supervision; an alternative is that contact transitions are often subframe events unseen at training frame rates. Use a "frame rate vs force-supervision density" two-arm study to separate the explanations.
reason: The headline (sampling aliasing as a WM error variable and variable Δt) is occupied by TAWM, and the prior-work review judges the difference "insufficiently certain" for clear-accept, capping novelty. The contact-specific, per-frame transition-probability scaling with an upsampling-versus-force-supervision two-arm separation has no single-paper hit, and the minimal falsification experiment is runnable in days on 1×H100, so it remains AwR.

idea: The standard account of adaptive-compute/early-exit VLA degradation on dynamic tasks is that difficult samples need full depth; an alternative is phase-dependent depth demand with overconfident contact transitions. Regress layerwise convergence depth on phase and confidence to separate the explanations.
reason: Each half of the headline is occupied by AC²-VLA (phase gating outperforms difficulty) and CEED-VLA (layerwise convergence-depth measurement). The prior-work review judges the combined diagnostic insufficient for clear-accept and a natural AC²-VLA extension, capping novelty; the decisive forward-only regression is inexpensive and runnable, so it remains AwR.

idea: The standard account of early saturation in frozen-VLA RL post-training with DSRL-style noise-space steering is sparse rewards/insufficient exploration; an alternative is a very low intrinsic dimension in the reachable steering action set, restricting movement to low-rank slices of the demonstration manifold. Increase steering rank vs densify rewards to separate the explanations.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap: no single paper occupies the low-rank reachable-set diagnostic, and its prescription opposes ZPRL, making it the strongest novelty of the three. However, the "steering has a ceiling" neighborhood is crowded (ZPRL/OGPO/LP-DS), the increment rests on one diagnostic, the prior-work review covers only arXiv without web/industry review, and no dimension reaches ≥8, so the ceiling is capped. The falsification experiment is executable but too heavy for a single-GPU study, so the verdict is AwR rather than SA.

idea: After distilling a VLA to edge scale, open-loop action error may match the teacher while closed-loop behavior develops oscillation/stagnation invisible to standard evaluation; quantify this hidden dynamics instability against student size and attribute it with a DAgger arm.
reason: The headline mechanism (open-loop parity/closed-loop instability) overlaps verified 2503.09722, and the prior-work review acknowledges the difference is insufficient for clear-accept, so novelty is capped (MAJOR). Uncertain identifier self-check adds a second MAJOR for weak prior-work review; however, the minimal falsification experiment is executable on one GPU in days (LIBERO with ≥50 rollouts × 3 seeds, an oscillation index and a DAgger attribution arm, and a clear signal), so the idea does not warrant rejection.

idea: Reevaluate leading VLAs under a fixed edge-inference budget (INT8+≤2 denoising steps, low resolution, and latency-capped replanning) and test whether leaderboard rankings change, asking whether current progress is measured outside any edge robot's executable regime.
reason: Edge budget reorders the leaderboard is a known method under a new configuration and does not reach SA by default. The core assumption is countered by verified BitVLA, which preserves performance under aggressive quantization, yielding one MAJOR; uncertain identifier self-check yields a second MAJOR. The minimal falsification experiment (full↔edge Kendall-τ, inference-only on one GPU, with τ≈1 as falsification) is executable, so it remains accept-w-rev.

idea: One demonstration for a redundant arm corresponds to an entire equivalent action manifold in joint null space; analytically sample Jacobian null-space motion for task-preserving augmentation and add a null-space-invariance loss, improving data efficiency and transfer across heterogeneous kinematic embodiments.
reason: Null-space augmentation is a mechanism transfer, and "task-preserving augmentation" is crowded, with verified RoCoDA occupation yielding one MAJOR; uncertain identifier self-check yields a second MAJOR, so novelty is capped. The minimal falsification experiment (Franka simulation±augmentation±invariance loss, jitter-arm control, transfer signal +10pt, runnable on one GPU) is executable, so it remains accept-w-rev.

idea: When vision-implied state contradicts force/proprioception-implied state (vision reports contact while force denial indicates penetration or localization error), the cross-modal disagreement itself is an unlabeled perception-fault detector for deployment-time early stopping and online auditing of VLM-judge rewards.
reason: Use vision↔force/proprioception learned cross-modal likelihood disagreement as an unlabeled detector. The minimal falsification experiment (pathology injection set, AUROC vs FIPER/Sentinel/residual FDI and removal of the cross-modal component) is executable and falsifiable; however, the diagnostic neighbor 2602.12032 creates gray-area overlap, and the difference cannot reach clear-accept, so novelty remains capped below SA.

idea: Visual forward passes are the most expensive step in edge VLA deployment, while force/proprioception are cheap and high-rate; formulate "compute-optimal sensor scheduling," using surprise in cheap modalities to decide whether to skip the visual backbone and treating vision as an expensive sensor sampled on demand.
reason: No single paper occupies the problem framing "use cheap-modality surprise to skip visual forward passes under compute-optimal per-sample sensor cost." The falsification experiment requires a Pareto signal of ≥40–50% fewer visual passes with force/phase-aligned trigger times and is executable and falsifiable; ElegantVLA (2605.29438) reuses motion cues for visual compute and is a substantive competitor, capping novelty below SA.

idea: Part of a teleoperator's action is driven by force felt at the hand, yet that force channel is usually neither recorded nor given to the policy, causing BC to attribute force-driven decisions to vision. Formalize this as unobserved confounding in the imitation target and quantify its systematic contact-phase bias.
reason: The minimal falsification signal, "contact-phase-selective gains," cannot distinguish the headline attribution "human-felt force is an unobserved BC confound" from published force-aware VLA (ForceVLA/FACTR) gains that already concentrate in contact (F6, the decisive experiment does not test the headline, MAJOR). Novelty also lies only in explanation/combination, while BRIDGE (phase selectivity)/force-conditioned/Causal-IL occupy the neighborhood; this is not a new method (F1, fragment-dominated, MAJOR).

idea: Construct a learned world model as a contact-implicit variational integrator that learns a discrete Lagrangian instead of directly predicting the next state, using structure preservation (momentum/symplectic structure) to eliminate long-horizon rollout drift.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.08279.

idea: Replace continuous action trajectories with self-supervised discrete contact-mode sequences; the VLA first plans mode transitions, then a low-level controller fills in continuous motion within each mode.
reason: The self-supervised two-level contact-mode representation has an executable falsification experiment and a handcrafted phase baseline, but DHAL/HDP/latent-action strongly occupy the three components, and the strongest counterexample DHAL was not opened. Novelty is capped below SA.

idea: Test whether physical-conservation residuals in a world model predict downstream model-based policy failures better than pixel/latent prediction errors, making physical fidelity a better WM selection signal.
reason: Conservation residuals vs prediction error as a WM selection signal is a clean, inexpensive, falsifiable diagnostic headline, but only 2 papers were fully read, the diagnostic ceiling is uncertain, and unverified novelty is capped.

idea: Use world-model coverage as a reward-free intrinsic signal to pretrain a noise-space steering policy for a frozen VLA, testing whether it reduces sample cost on later rewarded tasks.
reason: Reward-free frozen-VLA noise-space coverage pretraining has DSRL neighbors and a falsifiable design, but InDRiVE is nearly isomorphic (reward-free WM-disagreement pretraining), and the heavy stack requires multiple GPUs, capping it at AwR.

idea: Compile a language instruction once: a hypernetwork generates small policy weights from the instruction, then removes the language tower entirely at deployment; test whether weight-level conditioning improves compositional generalization over parameter-matched FiLM distillation.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.04898.

idea: Treat the physics engine as an unreported evaluation degree of freedom: reevaluate the same policies in two faithfully cross-ported simulation benchmarks, quantify ranking sensitivity to engine choice, and separate dynamics from rendering effects.
reason: 2606.10366 directly quantifies the headline "does ranking change with the engine" as a confound across three platforms. Under adverse judgment, the controlled-attribution difference may be insufficient for clear-accept, while the noise floor does not cover porting-artifact confounds; novelty is capped with 2 MAJOR findings.

idea: Measure a "demonstration replay ceiling" for every embodied benchmark: replay demonstrations under the benchmark's own success criterion, use the resulting success upper bound to correct SOTA saturation claims, and test whether replay failure predicts per-task BC degradation.
reason: The strongest counterexample, OpenVLA replay-filtering artifacts, was not opened; Semantic Scholar was rate-limited, and the search admits blind spots, so the weak prior-work review is a MAJOR finding and novelty remains unverified. The predictive-validity claim also fails to control task difficulty as a common cause, adding another MAJOR. Both headline implications are unoccupied, and the experiment is inexpensive and falsifiable, leaving it borderline.

idea: Evaluate action representations without training policies: build a training-free intrinsic suite for action tokenizers (round-trip reconstruction spectrum/closed-loop replay fidelity/perturbation amplification) and test whether intrinsic scores predict downstream VLA rankings.
reason: ActionCodec, the nearest neighbor, was not read even though its full text determines whether overlap increases, so adverse judgment yields one MAJOR. With n=12 representation points, the study also lacks power for the requirement that the bootstrap CI not overlap the single-metric MSE baseline, adding another MAJOR. The training-free suite and predictive-validity test itself is unoccupied across 8 fully read papers.

idea: Remove observation reconstruction as a default world-model objective; use value-equivalence/self-prediction objectives and test whether a robot world model without pixel reconstruction matches downstream control success from current reconstruction-based video-pretrained world models under equal data and compute.
reason: The headline wager (reconstruction is unnecessary/harmful) is partly supported by 2606.07687 (CALVIN) and MuDreamer (nearly isomorphic) +2406.17718, with medium overlap, so break-glass condition ① (zero hits) fails and SA is capped. The residual "equal-compute downstream-control head-to-head with causal ablation of the reconstruction auxiliary head" has no single-paper coverage, and the minimal falsification experiment is decisive, yielding a borderline verdict.

idea: Use similarity between force/tactile signatures and demonstrated contact prototypes to construct unlabeled dense rewards, replacing or supplementing VLM-judge so RL steering of a frozen VLA gets contact-phase rewards immune to visual interpenetration artifacts.
reason: The distance-to-demonstration reward scaffold is mature in OTR, and force is a modality swap; the three mechanism-transfer conditions, including nontrivial adaptation and a signal sufficient for clear-accept, fail, capping SA. The force-signature, frozen-VLA-steering, and interpenetration-motivation combination has no single-paper coverage, and falsification includes a decisive constant-amplitude attribution arm, yielding a borderline verdict.

idea: Use a VLA's forward model to predict the expected proprioception/force stream as an efference copy, re-encoding expensive vision only when measured-predicted deviation crosses a threshold, so sensor surprise triggers compute allocation.
reason: The adaptive-compute VLA umbrella is crowded (AC²-VLA/VLA-Cache/ElegantVLA/…), leaving only "gating on efference prediction error." Among 8 reads, only 2 were opened directly and 4 secondary competitors matched only by abstract, making the prior-work review weak under a crowded umbrella; those 2 MAJOR findings cap it. A rate-matched random attribution arm makes the kill test decisive, yielding a borderline verdict.

idea: When multiple embodiments demonstrate the same task, use phase-wise cross-embodiment trajectory variance to separate task-invariant subgoals from embodiment-specific execution; automatically extract an embodiment-agnostic subgoal skeleton as a training target for transfer to unseen embodiments.
reason: The headline (phase-wise cross-embodiment trajectory variance for segmentation with explicit skeleton supervision) has no single-paper coverage, but the broader proposition "separate task-invariant vs embodiment-specific execution" is occupied by XIRL/VLBiMan/BiDemoSyn, and the prior-work review judges the difference from XIRL insufficient for clear-accept, so novelty is capped. The minimal falsification experiment has text-subgoal and joint-training attribution arms and is executable on 1×H100; it is not a direct hit, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

idea: In mixed-source co-training, policies may identify data provenance implicitly and exploit source-specific shortcuts; formalize source leakage as hidden confounding and use a source-decoding probe with source-removal intervention to quantify damage to real transfer.
reason: The headline (data source as a hidden confound exploitable by the policy that systematically harms real transfer) is broadly asserted by 2508.06426 through its diversity/fragmentation framing (overlap medium-high). The I2 margin is only a diagnostic toolkit of a standard source-decoding probe, gradient-reversal source removal, and a placebo arm, not an independent contribution; the evidence-to-verdict path therefore leaves the F1 case dominated by close work.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that manipulation-video pretraining must depict hands or grippers acting on objects; test whether pure physical-event video without an actuator provides equally transferable dynamics priors under equal tokens.
reason: The load-bearing-assumption form fails both break-glass tests (low overlap-medium rather than low; only GR-2 supports the crack, while self-reported 2605.27817 was verified "not supporting"); the review therefore returns to the ordinary form. "Wager unverified" is one MAJOR, and partial support for "why removal is now possible" is another; however, no single paper covers the headline equal-token three-arm comparison, and the minimal falsification experiment is cheap, decisive, and runnable on 1×H100, capping the result at accept-w-rev.

idea: Use only compositional algebraic consistency of actions in latent space (associativity and returning to the start after do/undo) as the world model's sole self-supervised learning signal, without reconstruction or reward.
reason: The headline (composition and cycle latent-action algebra) is systematically anticipated by 2605.24578 at medium-high overlap. Only "algebra as the sole signal replacing reconstruction, the manipulation domain, and downstream control" remains uncovered, and the falsification experiment is decisive, capping the result at AwR.

idea: Desktop manipulation is approximately equivariant to scene SE(2); apply known transformations at deployment, measure whether actions transform accordingly, and use equivariance violations as a reward-free self-supervised signal for online test-time adaptation.
reason: Equivariance consistency as an online TTA loss is established by TransAdapt in vision, leaving I2 as domain transfer to VLA. Mechanism-transfer SA condition ② fails because the mechanism adaptation is nontrivial, but zero target-domain hits and a placebo falsification experiment support an AwR verdict.

idea: Remove the objective requiring action heads to regress recorded demonstration actions; instead use a frozen world model's effect-matching self-supervision, accepting any action predicted to achieve the demonstrated next-step geometric effect and making BC set-valued.
reason: Effect>recorded action is strongly anticipated by GLAM/State-Alignment at medium-high overlap. Assumption-removal SA condition ① fails because overlap is not low, and condition ② fails because only 1 crack supports the claim; the decisive falsification experiment leaves an ordinary-form AwR ceiling.

idea: Have the world model predict the object's final resting equilibrium in quasi-static manipulation rather than frame-by-frame transients, making outcome state rather than process the planning timescale.
reason: The minimal falsification experiment is decisive and runnable by one researcher on 1×H100, but the overlap is medium: 2411.04374 plans on equilibrium-state manifolds and 2509.21664 predicts stable placement states. The prior-work review judges the headline difference insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty below SA.

idea: Allocate policy reasoning depth by proximity to irreversibility: reason deeply and act slowly near release, pouring, or interference insertion; reason shallowly and act quickly in free space.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap, with a decisive random/difficulty-gating attribution arm, but difficulty-gating work such as GeCO already covers irreversible phases incidentally, leaving the increment entirely dependent on unverified causal separation. SA condition ③, that a realized signal suffices for clear-accept, fails, so the idea remains capped.

idea: A large share of cross-embodiment transfer failure may come from timescale mismatch rather than kinematics: the source embodiment can produce action bandwidth that the target cannot follow mechanically.
reason: The bandwidth-ratio and low-pass-repair framework is uncovered in the search (low overlap) and has an executable falsification experiment, but only 4 papers were fully read and the strongest neighbor, RichMap, was not read in full. The weak prior-work review is a MAJOR finding, novelty remains incompletely verified, and the idea is capped.

idea: Treat total energy (inference joules plus trajectory-motion joules) as a primary embodied-evaluation measure; test whether success-rate rankings reorder under a fixed energy budget and whether energy-optimal policies systematically sacrifice long-tail recovery.
reason: The main ranking-reorder contribution is measurement-heavy under the broad "evaluate policies by energy" framework established by The Price Is Not Right (2602.19260). The newer energy-recovery tension arm is downgraded to a weak attribution observation, and simulated energy proxies weaken the edge-deployment motivation, leaving insufficient SA ceiling. The minimal falsification experiment remains executable, with low overlap and feasibility for one researcher on 1×H100, reaching accept-w-rev.

idea: Propose compute-adaptive safety margins: when shared or edge compute is cut, the policy automatically widens spatial margins and slows down, trading speed for safety under compute starvation instead of collapsing under passive degradation.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap, with a strong decisive c-vs-b falsification design, but the learned mechanism in decisive arm c is underspecified. Feasible filtering/clipping risks reducing to the existing combination "CBF/HJ safety filtering and anytime slowing," and removing denoising steps may not validly simulate compute starvation. Oral/spotlight confidence is insufficient, so adverse judgment caps it at accept-w-rev.

idea: Bottleneck localization: for tiny edge policies failing high-precision tasks, the binding constraint may be action parameterization rather than parameter count; choosing the right action space could close more of the large-model gap than adding parameters.
reason: The prior-work review finds medium overlap and explicitly judges the difference "insufficiently certain" for clear-accept; the premise that "action space matters" is strongly established by 2602.23408, so the hard novelty-cap rule excludes SA. A parameterization×size interaction remains unoccupied, and the probe is executable and falsifiable (the wager dies if the interaction is insignificant), reaching accept-w-rev.

idea: Action-aligned narration in internet instructional/manipulation video contains physical information invisible to vision, including force, fragility, temperature, and compliance; use narration as free weak labels for force/contact/fragility in manipulation-video pretraining to restore contact-phase physical priors.
reason: Narration provides weak force/fragility/compliance labels has an executable minimal falsification experiment with a decisive "semantic arm vs physical arm" attribution. However, DeliGrasp/Feature Fields occupy the premise that language carries physical information, and the difference is not clear-accept-level, so novelty is capped below SA.

idea: Spontaneous speed profiles in first-person human video are maps of precision/caution demand (slow indicates fragile high-precision contact, while fast indicates free space). Current human-to-robot transfer keeps spatial paths but discards timing; predict and transfer tempo as free cross-embodiment supervision to prevent contact-phase failures.
reason: Tempo as a cross-embodiment caution map with shuffled attribution has no single-paper coverage and a decisive experiment, but the overlap is medium, the premise "slow indicates high precision" is firmly established in variable-impedance LfD/Fitts work, and the difference is uncertain. With no dimension reaching ≥7, so novelty is capped at borderline.

idea: Resampling source data with different control frequencies onto one time base during cross-embodiment co-training systematically aliases high-frequency contact transitions. Reinterpret part of negative transfer as a sampling-rate-aliasing data-pipeline artifact rather than embodiment kinematics, and separate the explanations with anti-aliasing resampling.
reason: Attributing part of negative transfer to sampling-rate aliasing with kinematics held fixed has low overlap and the strongest experimental design (probe score 5). However, the minimal falsification experiment is simulation-only and constructively creates aliasing, the prevalence in real OXE data is not directly falsified, and the repair is textbook, leaving insufficient evidence for SA.

idea: Formalize 1 supervisor serving N robots as an intervention queue: each robot uses expected wait time to decide when to request help and how to behave while waiting (safe hover/continue slowly/proceed at risk), then test whether queue awareness expands supervised fleet size by an order of magnitude under equal staffing.
reason: Robot-side queue-aware help requests occupy a low-overlap gap and have an executable, falsifiable experiment, but the prior-work review acknowledges the difference from the Fleet-DAgger dual is "insufficient (uncertain)" for clear-accept, capping novelty. The task-state cost behind the headline difference "hovering is not free" is also not instantiated in LIBERO/RoboCasa (1 MAJOR).

idea: Instruction wording for a frozen VLA is an unused free policy parameter: perform black-box wording search with a few deployment rollouts and quantify how much success pure language-side adaptation recovers with zero training and zero weight access.
reason: The headline mechanism (test-time black-box wording search as a deployment adaptation channel for a frozen VLA) is directly covered by 2606.12299 (rollout-feedback wording search with a black-box frozen VLA, LIBERO, and a real robot) and 2602.12281 (verifier-selected rewrites). The prior-work review judges high overlap and falsifies the "why it may be new" premise; F1 is dominated by close work and therefore CRITICAL.

idea: Increase manipulation-world-model observation context by an order of magnitude, use the "persistence half-life" of contained/occluded objects as a primary estimand, and compare the persistence-compute frontier of longer attention windows against compressed fixed-size state.
reason: No single paper covers the headline combination (container-hidden-state half-life estimand and same-compute four-architecture×context frontier), but all three components are separately occupied and three close 2026 papers are converging quickly. The prior-work review acknowledges the difference from On Memory is "insufficient (uncertain)," capping novelty, and the "same compute" matching protocol is undefined (1 MAJOR).

idea: Treat embodied reward/VLM-judge metrics as the evaluated object: build a simulated perception-pathology diagnostic set with known truth—interpenetration false completions, occluded true completions, and threshold flicker—and measure each reward signal's false positives, false negatives, and temporal stability rather than policy quality.
reason: Recheck raises overlap to medium: RoboReward (2601.00675) +2606.01036 occupy most of the headline. The residual (simulation-pathology taxonomy and temporal stability) lacks independent evidence for clear accept, capping novelty; validity of a simulation-artifact certificate when extrapolated to real-robot evaluation adds one MAJOR.

idea: Human-in-the-loop deployment evaluation treats intervention rate/autonomous duration as objective, but the person pressing the intervention key is an uncalibrated measuring instrument. Quantify how much inter-supervisor variance explains cross-method gaps and define a reproducible standardized synthetic-supervisor protocol.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap, but this is a mechanism transfer of the rater-calibration paradigm in 2602.22585 to HIL, with insufficient evidence for SA conditions ① and ③. A video proxy cannot measure online intervention variance (MAJOR), while HRI/human-factors venues were not searched, making prior-work review weak (MAJOR), so the idea is capped.

idea: VLA has no validation set: checkpoints are selected by test-task success. Formalize this overlooked "winner's curse" confound as selection bias analytically predictable from evaluation noise, then quantify its share of reported gains.
reason: The headline phenomenon was named and measured by 2606.04233 (best/final gap 0.6–4.4pp), while RouterVLA measures 1.87×; the residual is analytical/audit refinement with capped novelty. The claim about "share of reported gains" is not identifiable under the current design, adding one MAJOR.

idea: Negative transfer in cross-embodiment co-training may concentrate in contact phases with the largest embodiment-dynamics differences rather than uniformly; decompose negative transfer by manipulation phase and predict its location from phase-level dynamics divergence.
reason: The prior-work review finds low overlap, but the headline mechanism (divergence predicts transfer) is occupied by 2003.13150, while the increment only moves to phase granularity and its expected direction is qualitative consensus in the adjacent sim2real domain (2311.07499). The prior-work review judges the difference insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty; the minimal falsification experiment is executable and falsifiable, so this remains borderline rather than reject.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that every robot in a fleet must deploy the same best checkpoint: training byproduct checkpoints provide natural behavioral diversity, and heterogeneous deployment turns the data flywheel into free population-level exploration while reducing correlated failures in a homogeneous fleet.
reason: The assumption-removal form fails break-glass condition ① because zero headline hits is unproven (medium overlap), and condition ② has only 1 supporting crack, so ordinary evaluation applies. The two mechanism halves are occupied by 1802.08331 (diverse policy populations collect data, with theory)/2412.04305, while failure decorrelation is occupied by 2101.05853, capping novelty. The minimal falsification experiment includes an action-noise placebo arm and can decisively kill the wager, so it remains accept-w-rev.

idea: Human interventions are treated as homogeneous corrective demonstrations, but an intervention may intend to change strategy, refine precision, or recover. Classify intent automatically from signal form and route each type to a different learning update, extracting more improvement from the same human budget.
reason: The headline is densely occupied around all sides (RaC protocol-level classification into 2 types/e-stop as one type/intent inference changes the update/nonuniform treatment by signal), with medium overlap and insufficient difference for clear-accept. In phase-1, scripted supervisor intent serves as truth, so recoverability of the three intervention types in real data, the core value claim, is untestable and counts as a second MAJOR finding; 2 MAJOR findings therefore cap the idea on construct validity. A permutation placebo arm can falsify routing, so the result is capped at accept-w-rev.

idea: The timestamp of a human intervention is a delayed noisy label for the failure boundary; autonomous actions in the preceding 0.5–2 seconds are systematically treated as neutral samples, contaminating policy retraining.
reason: Sirius identifies the headline problem (systematic mislabeling in the pre-takeover τ window) and uses a fixed window hard-zero correction. I1 differs only in method granularity (τ-distribution estimation and soft relabeling with a delay kernel), and the fully read prior-work evidence indicates that the difference is insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty.

idea: Test whether removing the VLM language tower and using only a visual encoder and thin task embedding matches the full VLM on manipulation tasks requiring language distinctions, localizing whether language grounding is load-bearing.
reason: Probe evidence such as RoboSemanticBench occupies the claim "VLA does not use language/language grounding is weak" (≈arm C). The only new equal-compute tower-removal arm B has an invalid criterion because task-id leakage gives oracle identity while unseen instructions have no input channel.

idea: Make a discrete contact-mode transition graph the primary prediction target of a manipulation world model instead of pixels/latent state, treating nonsmooth mode transitions as a first-class supervision target.
reason: Contact-event prediction and conditioned dynamics exist in Contact-Aware Neural Dynamics (2601.12796). The I2 increment only reverses primary and auxiliary objectives, using a 4-state mode graph as the primary target and mode-F1 as a selection signal; the prior-work review leans toward insufficient clear-accept difference without evidence, capping novelty.

idea: Remove the assumption that RL steering of a frozen generative policy must operate in noise-seed space and that noise seeds form a sufficient control surface; compare reachable behavior sets across frozen control surfaces under equal budget, falsifying sufficiency if another surface strictly dominates noise seeds.
reason: Break-glass condition ② fails because only 1 crack supports the claim, so SA is unavailable. LP-DS reports noise>action, opposite the premise, and the difference is insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty. Head-to-head comparison across surfaces confounds optimizer differences, yielding 2 MAJOR findings.

idea: A teleoperator's gaze locks onto the next target about 0.5-1 s before hand motion, but most teleoperation data discard this free prospective-intent signal; inject gaze as lead-time weak supervision for subgoals/intent in long-horizon VLAs.
reason: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: GazeVLA (future-window gaze injection with gaze-free testing), and TAVIS (lead time already measured) nearly cover the headline, leaving only an ablation-level increment. Long-horizon data with gaze require custom collection beyond the fixed single-researcher resources, yielding 2 MAJOR findings.

idea: Treat the few scene entities a manipulation policy truly depends on within a time window as its "working set" and measure reference locality; test whether failures concentrate at working-set churn (thrashing) near subtask boundaries rather than on difficult samples, then stabilize the set with boundary prefetching.
reason: The broader proposition is occupied by Tri-Info/SAFE/Foresight, while the minimal falsification experiment does not compare incremental predictive power against them and only controls the difficulty confound. 2603.11642 already occupies an intervention on boundary failures and is mis-cited by the idea; mechanism transfer lacks nontrivial adaptation, yielding 2 MAJOR findings.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that visual input must be a fixed-frame-rate stream: data, architecture, and simulation stacks all treat frames as atomic; use a native event-stream manipulation policy and test under an equal bandwidth budget whether the frame abstraction is truly load-bearing.
reason: Prescreening found a direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.17643.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that long-horizon manipulation requires explicit hierarchy/subgoals: if a flat long-context policy matches a hierarchical policy under equal supervision and compute, hierarchy's benefit is confounded by subgoal supervision rather than structure.
reason: Half of the headline, "flat matches hierarchical," is occupied by 2505.14975/2606.10267, so novelty is capped; the third arm's "same subgoal supervision" has asymmetric test-time subgoal conditioning, making the confound separation questionable, which is a MAJOR finding. The residual "structure vs supervision" confound decomposition may be unoccupied, and the falsification experiment is decisive and feasible, so the verdict is AwR.

idea: Treat human intervention as a label censored by operator bias: operators intervene incorrectly in states that look dangerous but would succeed, and these false-positive corrections teach excessive conservatism; quantify the effect and repair it by filtering with privileged continuations.
